After Phase 1 — You Will Be Able To:
✦ Open any client's QBO and know exactly where everything is — in under 60 seconds
✦ Explain the Bank Feed to a client and tell them exactly why wrong entries corrupt their financial statements
✦ Sort any receipt into the right bucket — Business Expense, Personal Draw, or Asset — including the tricky ones
✦ Tell your client why their ₱32,000 laptop is on the Balance Sheet — not the P&L — and why that matters for BIR
✦ Process a full January for a service business — 15 transactions, complete COA, bank reconciliation, verified P&L
✦ Know which BIR documents to keep for each transaction — OR, 2307, bank statement — and why each one matters
🎙️

"Most students finish Phase 1 and say: I wish I had this before I started working with clients. You're getting it now. Do the work." — Mic

💬 Before you start — a quick note from Mic
👆 Click my photo to hear a personal message 🎧

You might be wondering: "Is this as good as having a live teacher?"

Honest answer: a live teacher explains the theory. The Practice HUB makes you practice until it becomes instinct. When you sit with a real client for the first time, you won't be nervous — because you've already done this before.

Every wrong answer explains exactly why it's wrong. Every transaction has a BIR note. Every phase ends with a real QBO task you execute yourself. This is not a video course. This is a flight simulator.

👤 Personal / Owner's Draw

Drop receipts here

A Message from Mic — Read This First
Senior Bookkeeper · The Practice HUB

Hey! Before you dive in — take a breath. 😊 This Practice Set is where the real learning happens. You're about to do what most bookkeeping students never do: actually sit down, open QBO, and record real transactions for a real business.

Follow the tabs in this exact order:

① QBO Setup — Set up your QBO Practice File first. Always.
② Chart of Accounts — Create every account before any transaction.
③ Contacts — Add all customers and suppliers first.
④ Opening Balances — Enter Patricia's Jan 1 starting numbers.
⑤ January — 15 transactions. Try first, then Reveal.
⑥ February — Harder. Credit notes, withholding tax, depreciation.
⑦ Answer Keys — Unlocks after all 30 items. That's intentional.
💡 The Reveal button is not cheating — try first, reveal, and understand WHY. That's how learning locks in.
⚠️ Don't skip setup tabs. Wrong COA = wrong books, no matter how carefully you enter transactions.

"You're not just practicing bookkeeping. You're building the confidence to say — I've done this before. I know what I'm doing. Take your time. I'm right here." — Mic 🌟

🏢 About the Business
Business Name:Reyes Virtual Assistant Services
Owner:Patricia A. Reyes
Business Type:Sole Proprietorship
Services:Admin support, social media mgmt, bookkeeping assistance
Address:Las Piñas City, Metro Manila
Bank:UnionBank Digital Business Account
BIR Status:NON-VAT Registered (below ₱3M threshold)
📖 How to Use This Practice Set
1Setup & Info — Review Setup, COA, Contacts, and Opening Balances.
2January Transactions — Enter all 15 transactions into your QBO.
3February Transactions — Handle harder tasks like credit memos and tricky traps.
4Bank Recon & Answer Key — Unlocks when 30 items are completed!
🇵🇭 BIR Note: Patricia is NON-VAT registered (annual revenue below ₱3,000,000). She issues Official Receipts (OR) — not VAT Official Receipts — for all services rendered. For expenses, record the FULL AMOUNT even if the supplier's receipt shows VAT.
🗺️ QBO Step-by-Step Setup
  • Go to Gear ⚙️ > Account and Settings
  • Company Name: Reyes Virtual Assistant Services
  • Company Type: Sole Proprietorship
  • Sales: Turn on "Custom transaction numbers"
  • Advanced: Accounting method set to Accrual
📗 Chart of Accounts
  • UnionBank Digital Business (Bank / Cash and cash equivalents)
  • Accounts Receivable (A/R) (Accounts Receivable)
  • Office Equipment (Fixed Asset / Machinery and Equipment)
  • Patricia Reyes, Capital (Equity / Owner's Equity)
  • Patricia Reyes, Drawings (Equity / Owner's Equity)
  • Virtual Assistant Services (Income / Service/Fee Income)
  • Internet Expense (Expense / Utilities)
  • Office Supplies (Expense / Office/General Administrative Expenses)
  • Software / Dues (Expense / Dues and subscriptions)
  • Bank Charges (Expense / Bank charges)
  • Interest Income (Income / Interest Income)
👥 Contacts

Customers: Andres Cruz Deliveries, Carmela Santos, BG Events Planning Corp., Lovely Reyes (Lovely's Boutique)

Suppliers: PLDT Inc., Office Warehouse, Meralco, PowerMac Center, Shopee, Lazada, Canva, Microsoft Philippines

📘 Opening Balances (As of Jan 1, 2025)
  • UnionBank Digital Business: ₱50,000.00 (Debit)
  • Office Equipment: ₱25,000.00 (Debit)
  • Patricia Reyes, Capital: ₱75,000.00 (Credit)
📅 January 2025 — 15 Transactions
For each transaction: figure out the account → enter in QBO → tick Done ✓. Click 💡 Reveal Account only after you've tried!
Date#DescriptionAmountAccountDone in QBO
📅 February 2025 — 15 Transactions
Level Up
Level up: VAT identification, credit notes, withholding tax, bank entries.
⭐ February is harder. Watch for: VAT on receipts (you are non-VAT — expense the FULL amount including VAT), credit notes that REDUCE income, corporate EWT, bank-generated entries with no invoice, and one very sneaky personal expense trap.
Date#DescriptionAmountAccountDone in QBO
🔒
Answer Key Locked
Complete all 30 transactions (January & February) to unlock the full P&L and Bank Recon answer key.
📊 Expected P&L — Reyes Virtual Assistant Services, Jan-Feb 2025
Revenue (2 Months)
Virtual Assistant Services₱65,000
Interest Income₱37
Total Revenue₱65,037
Operating Expenses (2 Months)
Internet Expense(₱3,198)
Utilities Expense(₱2,400)
Office Supplies(₱1,800)
Software / Dues(₱800)
Bank Charges(₱200)
Total Expenses(₱8,398)
Net Income — YTD 2025
₱56,639.00
🏦 Bank Reconciliation Check (Feb 28)
Starting Balance (Jan 1)₱50,000.00
Ending Bank Balance (Feb 28)₱36,539.00
⚠️ Why didn't the Grab trip or the Ergonomic Chair affect the P&L?
The Office Chair (₱4,500) is an Asset. The Grab trip to MOA (₱450) is personal and goes to Owner's Draw (Equity). The PLDT bill (₱1,299) was expensed entirely — because Patricia is NON-VAT, she cannot claim input VAT, so the full amount is her expense.
🎯
If your Net Income shows exactly ₱56,639.00 in QBO — you nailed it. Every peso accounts for. Every transaction in the right place.
🎉
You Did It.

You just processed 30 real transactions for Reyes Virtual Assistant Services across two full months — January and February 2025. Most people who say they want to learn bookkeeping never get this far.

RESOURCE 1 · PDF GUIDE
10 Common QBO Mistakes Beginners Make
Avoid the most common traps. This guide shows you exactly what they are — and how to fix them before they mess up your client's books.
❌ Categorizing personal expenses as business
❌ Ignoring uncategorized bank feed items
❌ Expensing assets immediately (big one!)
❌ Editing reconciled transactions
❌ Using wrong expense categories
❌ Skipping bank reconciliation every month
✅ Each mistake has the fix inside
✅ Real client warnings + Mic Tips
🎙️ Mic Tip: Keep this PDF handy when you open a new QBO account. It's completely beginner-friendly, highly visual, and contains zero confusing accounting jargon.

Free Starter Pack · The Practice HUB
Beginner Bookkeeper Starter Pack

You don't need a degree. You don't need to already know everything. You just need a place to start — and this is it. Confusion is normal. Bookkeeping is learnable. You can do this.

Every bookkeeper you admire was once exactly where you are. The difference between someone who became confident and someone who gave up? They kept going. Confusion is training, not failure.
Section A
Beginner Roadmap
What to learn first and the exact sequence that makes sense
Section B
QBO Navigation Cheat Sheet
Banking, COA, Sales, Expenses, Reports — where to click
Section C
Bookkeeping Terms Glossary
13 essential terms in plain English with real-world analogies
Section D
Sample Workflow
Full bookkeeping cycle from receipt to report
Section E
Mini Practice Exercise
Fake business, 5 transactions, 1 tricky trap, answer key
Bonus
Tips & Next Steps
Mistakes, confidence reminders, what to practice next
One tip before you begin: Go at your own pace. There's no rush. Bookmark what confuses you and come back — that's real learning.
Your Beginner Roadmap

Think of this as a GPS, not a race. Here's the order that actually makes sense before you open a real client's QBO.

Skip these for now — they're advanced: Payroll · Inventory management · VAT setup · Advanced journal entries · Multi-currency · Cleanup projects · Reconciling 3+ months at once
Mentor tip from Mic: You don't need to know everything before you start. Every great bookkeeper learned the same basics first. The advanced stuff makes more sense when you get there.

The Bookkeeping Cycle

Mini Practice Exercise

You're the bookkeeper for Kara's Bloom Studio — October 2024. Categorize 5 transactions. One is a trap.

Your task: Click the correct category for each transaction. One is intentionally tricky — look out for it!
Sample receipts
National BookstoreOct 3, 2024
Notebook (3 pcs)₱195.00
Pens (1 pack)₱75.00
TOTAL₱270.00
Globe TelecomOct 7, 2024
Monthly broadband — October₱1,499.00
TOTAL₱1,499.00
Davao Flower MarketOct 12, 2024
Rose bundles × 20₱3,600.00
Baby's breath × 10₱800.00
TOTAL₱4,400.00
Tricky transaction — look carefully at Transaction 5. Kara transferred ₱5,000 from her personal bank account into the business. How is this recorded?
This is an Owner's Contribution — NOT income. When the owner puts personal money into the business, it's recorded under Equity ("Owner's Investment" or "Owner's Contribution"). Not Revenue — the business didn't earn it from a client. This is the most common beginner trap.
Common Mistakes, Confidence Reminders & What's Next
Common beginner mistakes
⚠️
Miscategorizing as "Misc"
Be specific. Specific categories make reports useful.
⚠️
Skipping reconciliation
Categorizing isn't enough. Reconciliation proves your numbers.
⚠️
Mixing personal & business
Owner's personal spending = Owner's Draw, never a business expense.
⚠️
Trusting QBO blindly
QBO guesses. You verify. Always review before clicking "Add."
Confidence reminders
⭐
Mistakes are training
Every error you fix teaches you something real.
⭐
You don't need to know everything first
Start simple. Grow knowledge as you work.
⭐
Confusion means you're learning
That uncomfortable feeling goes away if you keep going.
⭐
Organization beats speed
Clients value accuracy. Speed comes with practice.
What to practice next
Final note from Mic: You came here as a beginner. That takes courage. Bookkeeping is learnable and the industry needs more Filipino bookkeepers who are careful, organized, and dedicated. That can be you. Keep going.
